title: 10 best herbal tinctures for periodontal disease
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for periodontal disease are concentrated liquid extracts of medicinal herbs, used to treat gum infections and inflammation. They offer a natural alternative to conventional treatments, with fewer side effects. Examples include Goldenseal, which has antibacterial properties, Myrrh, known for its anti-inflammatory effects, and Echinacea, which boosts the immune system. By promoting gum health, these tinctures can alleviate pain, reduce swelling, and improve overall oral hygiene. This not only enhances patients' quality of life but also contributes to a better sense of well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's astringent properties can also help to tighten and heal gum tissue, reducing the depth of periodontal pockets and promoting overall oral health. Additionally, yarrow has been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which can help to speed up the healing process and prevent further infection. When used as a mouthwash or added to toothpaste, yarrow tincture can provide a natural and effective way to manage periodontal disease and promote healthy gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce gum inflammation, one of the main symptoms of periodontal disease, due to the presence of flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of periodontal disease-causing bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis and Aggregatibacter actinomycetemcomitans, thanks to the active constituents like alpha-bisabolol and azulenes.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property helps tighten and constrict gum tissues, reducing gaps between teeth and gums where bacteria can thrive, due to the tannins present in the plant.

Analgesic: The analgesic property of yarrow tincture alleviates gum pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease, as it contains sesquiterpene lactones and essential oils with known pain-relieving effects.

Wound healing: Yarrow tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, which is crucial for treating periodontal disease, as it contains flavonoids and alkaloids that stimulate cell proliferation and tissue repair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides.
Chop the yarrow into small pieces and place in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it three-quarters full.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-proof vodka or rum over the chopped yarrow, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily.

Strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a dark glass dropper bottle, discarding the spent plant material. The resulting tincture can be used directly or diluted with water as needed for periodontal disease treatment.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's mucilage content also helps to soothe and heal damaged gum tissues, while its astringent properties can tighten and strengthen gums. Additionally, marshmallow tincture can stimulate the production of saliva, which helps to wash away food particles and bacteria that can contribute to gum disease. Overall, the healing properties of marshmallow tincture can support oral health and help to prevent and treat periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and redness in the gums, which are common symptoms of periodontal disease. This property is attributed to the mucilage content in marshmallow root.

Antimicrobial: Marshmallow tincture's antimicrobial property helps inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to periodontal disease. The antimicrobial effects are due to the presence of tannins and phenolic acids.
Wound healing: Marshmallow tincture's wound healing property promotes tissue regeneration and helps repair damaged gums. This is attributed to the mucilage and flavonoid content in marshmallow root.

Analgesic: Marshmallow tincture's analgesic property helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with periodontal disease. This property is due to the presence of various compounds, including triterpene saponins.

Anti-oxidant: Marshmallow tincture's anti-oxidant property helps protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals. This property is attributed to the presence of various antioxidant compounds, including phenolic acids and flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried marshmallow root into small pieces and combine with 2 cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids, reserving the liquid.
Store the liquid in a clean, airtight container in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
Use 1-2 mL of the tincture up to three times daily by adding it to water or taking it directly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kinnikinnick tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce gum inflammation, fight harmful bacteria, and promote gum tissue healing. The herbal blend found in kinnikinnick tincture, including oak, willow bark, and other natural ingredients, can help soothe irritated gums, reduce pocket depth, and improve overall oral health. By using kinnikinnick tincture as a complementary treatment, individuals with periodontal disease may experience a decrease in gum inflammation, bleeding, and other symptoms associated with the condition. Its natural healing properties can support the body's natural defenses and contribute to healthier gums and teeth.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Kinnikinnick tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce gum inflammation, a common symptom of periodontal disease, due to the presence of tannins and essential oils.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial property aids in fighting off periodontal disease-causing bacteria, thanks to the presence of volatile oils and alkaloids.
Astringent: The astringent property of kinnikinnick tincture helps tighten gum tissues, reducing pocket depths and promoting healing, due to the presence of tannins.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of kinnikinnick tincture provides pain relief from periodontal disease symptoms, attributed to the presence of alkaloids and essential oils.

Circulation-enhancing: Kinnikinnick tincture's circulation-enhancing property improves blood flow to the gums, aiding in the healing process and preventing further infection, due to the presence of essential oils and tannins.
remedy_recipe: Chop one cup of herbs including bayberry bark, echinacea root, and myrrh gum to release their properties, then combine in a clean jar.
Pour 2 cups of 100-proof alcohol or apple cider vinegar over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged, and seal the jar tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days to help the extraction process.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the spent herbs.

Transfer the resulting tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle, storing it in a cool, dark place or refrigerator for up to one year. Use 2-3 drops of the tincture on the affected gum area, three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The herb, also known as Baptisia tinctoria, has been traditionally used in Native American medicine to treat various infections. Its active compounds, such as baptisin and baptisin-glucoside, inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth that can lead to gum disease. Additionally, wild indigo tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a key factor in the progression of periodontal disease. By promoting oral health and reducing inflammation, wild indigo tincture can help manage periodontal disease and improve overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild indigo tincture can help reduce harmful bacteria in the mouth, which is important for periodontal disease as it is caused by bacterial infection and inflammation. The antimicrobial properties are attributed to the alkaloids present in the plant, such as indigo and indirubin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Wild indigo tincture can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which is crucial for periodontal disease as it is characterized by inflammation and swelling of the gums. The anti-inflammatory properties are attributed to the isoflavones and other polyphenolic compounds present in the plant.

Analgesic properties: Wild indigo tincture can help relieve pain associated with periodontal disease. This is important as the disease can cause discomfort and pain during chewing or brushing. The analgesic properties are attributed to the alkaloids present in the plant.

Immunostimulant properties: Wild indigo tincture can help boost the immune system, which is important for periodontal disease as it is a chronic inflammatory condition that requires a strong immune response. The immunostimulant properties are attributed to the polysaccharides present in the plant.

Antioxidant properties: Wild indigo tincture can help reduce oxidative stress in the mouth, which is important for periodontal disease as it is associated with increased oxidative stress. The antioxidant properties are attributed to the flavonoids and other polyphenolic compounds present in the plant.
remedy_recipe: Harvest wild indigo (Baptisia tinctoria) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pesticides, then rinse them gently.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of fresh herbs or 1 ounce of dried herbs and place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of 80-100 proof vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.

Strain the tincture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store it in a dark glass bottle with a dropper. Use 1-2 dropperfuls (20-40 drops) up to three times a day for periodontal disease.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with periodontal disease because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb's active compounds, including flavonoids and carotenoids, can reduce inflammation in the gums, fight harmful bacteria that cause gum infections, and promote tissue regeneration. By using pot marigold tincture as a complementary treatment, individuals with periodontal disease may experience improved gum health, reduced pocket depth, and a decreased risk of tooth loss. This natural remedy can be used alongside conventional therapies to enhance the overall treatment outcome.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in periodontal tissues, which is important because inflammation is a major contributor to periodontal disease; active constituents include flavonoids and essential oils.

Antimicrobial: The tincture inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, crucial for controlling periodontal disease; active constituents include volatile oils, such as limonene and α-pinene.

Wound healing: Pot marigold tincture promotes the healing of damaged gum tissue, important for repairing periodontal tissue; active constituents include tannins and flavonoids, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Analgesic: The tincture helps alleviate pain associated with periodontal disease; active constituents include salicylates, which are known to have pain-relieving properties.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tincture contains antioxidants that help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress, important for preventing periodontal disease progression; active constituents include carotenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 cups of fresh pot marigold flowers (Calendula officinalis) or 1 cup of dried flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or rum over the flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Securely cover the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers and transferring the liquid to a clean glass bottle or jar.
Label the tincture with the ingredients, preparation date, and recommended dosage (e.g., 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day) for periodontal disease use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can reduce gum inflammation, fight harmful bacteria, and promote tissue repair. The herb's active compounds, including asiaticoside, madecassoside, and madasiatic acid, have been shown to improve collagen synthesis, which is essential for maintaining healthy gums and preventing periodontal disease progression. Moreover, gotu kola's astringent properties can help tighten gum tissue, reduce bleeding, and minimize the risk of infection. Its potential to enhance wound healing and tissue regeneration makes it a valuable adjunct in the treatment and management of periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Gotu kola tincture can help reduce bacteria in the mouth that cause periodontal disease, thanks to its active constituents such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and betulinic acid.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Gotu kola's ability to decrease inflammation helps alleviate symptoms of periodontal disease, as it contains triterpenoids that inhibit the release of inflammatory mediators.

Wound healing properties: The tincture promotes the healing of gum tissue due to its active constituents, including asiatic acid and madecassic acid, which stimulate collagen production and tissue regeneration.

Antioxidant properties: Gotu kola's high antioxidant content helps protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by periodontal disease, with key antioxidant compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Vasoprotective properties: The tincture's ability to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation supports gum health and can help reduce the progression of periodontal disease, owing to its active constituents such as triterpene saponins and triterpene glycosides.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried gotu kola herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, amber glass bottle, away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb's active compounds, including chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, have been shown to reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria that can cause gum disease. Additionally, the tincture can help stimulate the production of collagen, a protein that is essential for maintaining healthy gums and tissue. By using eastern purple coneflower tincture as a natural supplement, individuals may be able to improve their oral health and reduce the risk of periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which is important for periodontal disease as it can alleviate pain, swelling, and bleeding associated with the disease. This property is attributed to the presence of chamazulene and other sesquiterpene lactones.

Antimicrobial property: The tincture can help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, which is crucial for periodontal disease management as it can prevent the progression of gum infection and inflammation. The active constituents responsible for this property include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils.

Analgesic property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture has pain-relieving properties, which is important for periodontal disease as it can help alleviate discomfort caused by gum inflammation and tissue damage. This property is attributed to the presence of alkamides and other volatile oils.

Antioxidant property: The tincture can help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is important for periodontal disease as it can promote healing and tissue regeneration. The antioxidant property is attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds, such as caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid.

Immunomodulatory property: Eastern purple coneflower tincture can help regulate the immune response, which is important for periodontal disease as it can reduce the overactive immune response that contributes to gum inflammation and tissue damage. This property is attributed to the presence of polysaccharides and other complex carbohydrates.
remedy_recipe: Harvest purple coneflower (Echinacea purpurea) flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully grown and free from pests or disease, typically in late summer or early fall.
Chop or grind the harvested plant material to increase its surface area, then fill a clean, dry jar tightly with the herb, leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Pour 100-proof vodka or another food-grade alcohol into the jar, completely covering the plant material and reaching the one-inch mark above it, ensuring a 1:2 herb-to-menstruum ratio.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, gently shaking the mixture once a day or as often as possible.

Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine mesh strainer, squeezing the plant material to extract all liquid, and store the final product in a dark glass bottle, away from direct sunlight. Recommended dosage for periodontal disease is 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Teaberry tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compounds in teaberry, such as ursolic acid and gallic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth that can lead to gum disease. Additionally, teaberry tincture can help reduce inflammation in the gums, which is a common symptom of periodontal disease. The astringent properties of teaberry can also help to tighten and strengthen gum tissue, promoting overall oral health. By using teaberry tincture as a natural remedy, individuals can help to prevent and treat periodontal disease, while also avoiding the potential side effects of conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Teaberry tincture contains compounds like berberine and ursolic acid that help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, reducing the risk of periodontal disease.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of tannins and flavonoids in teaberry tincture helps decrease inflammation in the gums, alleviating symptoms associated with periodontal disease.
Analgesic properties: Teaberry tincture has pain-relieving properties due to its eugenol content, which can help manage discomfort caused by periodontal disease.
Astringent properties: The astringent nature of teaberry tincture, attributed to its tannins, helps tighten gum tissue, reducing gum pockets and promoting oral health.

Antioxidant properties: Teaberry tincture is rich in antioxidants like phenolic acids, which combat oxidative stress and support gum tissue regeneration, aiding in the treatment of periodontal disease.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh teaberry leaves and stems, place them in a clean jar, then add 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol.
Tightly seal the jar, shake it well, then store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle or jar.
Label the bottle with the ingredients, date, and recommended dosage (e.g., 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day).
Store the teaberry tincture in a cool, dark place, where it can last for several years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wood avens tincture helps with periodontal disease because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria in the mouth. The herb's active compound, coumarin, has been shown to inhibit the growth of periodontal pathogens, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. Additionally, wood avens tincture can stimulate the production of collagen, a protein that is essential for the health of the gums and other tissues in the mouth. By promoting collagen production, wood avens tincture can help to strengthen the gums and prevent further damage caused by periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wood avens tincture contains antimicrobial constituents, such as tannins and essential oils, that help control the growth of periodontal disease-causing bacteria, reducing infection and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory properties of wood avens tincture, attributed to its phenolic compounds, help alleviate periodontal tissue inflammation and swelling, promoting gum health.

Astringent properties: Wood avens tincture's astringent action, due to its tannin content, helps tighten and strengthen periodontal tissues, reducing gum pocket depth and preventing further tissue damage.

Wound healing properties: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, thanks to its procyanidin content, which encourages the growth of new connective tissue and blood vessels in the periodontium.

Antioxidant properties: Wood avens tincture's antioxidant constituents, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, protect periodontal tissues from oxidative stress and damage, supporting overall gum health and preventing disease progression.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wood avens root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herbs until they are completely covered.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 1-2 dropperfuls daily for periodontal disease.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tincture helps with periodontal disease because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It contains berberine, a compound that reduces inflammation and fights harmful bacteria in the mouth. This herbal tincture can help soothe irritated gums, reduce gum bleeding, and promote gum tissue healing. Additionally, goldenseal tincture can help prevent plaque buildup and protect against oral infections, all of which contribute to periodontal disease. Its potent healing properties make it a valuable addition to any oral health regimen aimed at managing and preventing periodontal disease.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Goldenseal tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce inflammation in the gums, a common symptom of periodontal disease, due to its active constituents, such as berberine and canadine.

Antimicrobial: Goldenseal tincture's antimicrobial property inhibits the growth of harmful bacteria in the mouth, contributing to periodontal disease, thanks to its active constituents, including berberine and hydrastine.
Astringent: Goldenseal tincture's astringent property tightens and strengthens gum tissues, promoting healing and reducing the risk of periodontal disease progression, due to its tannins content.

Immune-boosting: Goldenseal tincture's immune-boosting property supports the body's defense mechanisms, helping to combat periodontal disease and promote oral health, mainly due to its alkaloids, such as berberine.

Wound-healing: Goldenseal tincture's wound-healing property promotes the healing of damaged gum tissues, reducing the risk of periodontal disease progression, thanks to its active constituents, including berberine and hydrastine.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried goldenseal root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of 100-proof vodka or grain alcohol over the goldenseal, ensuring all pieces are covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle and use 1-2 dropperfuls daily for periodontal disease.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat periodontal disease with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of anti-inflammatory and antibacterial herbs. For instance, goldenseal and myrrh have potent antibacterial properties that can help reduce gum inflammation and fight off harmful bacteria. Additionally, calendula and tea tree oil have anti-inflammatory effects that can soothe irritated gums and promote healing. Dilute the tinctures in water and rinse your mouth with the solution twice a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional. However, it's important to note that while herbal tinctures can complement traditional periodontal treatments, they should never replace professional dental care.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to periodontal disease that are treated with herbal tinctures include gingivitis and loose teeth. Gingivitis, an early stage of gum disease, can be managed with herbal remedies like goldenseal and myrrh tinctures, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Myrrh, in particular, can help reduce gum inflammation and strengthen gums. For loose teeth, a tincture made from natural ingredients like echinacea, calendula, and plantain can be used to promote tissue repair and reduce infection. These herbal tinctures can help alleviate symptoms and improve oral health when used in conjunction with regular dental care and professional treatment. However, it's important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment regimen.
